description_long: This object is a large, antique wooden camera, reminiscent of early box cameras used in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. The camera features a rectangular wooden body with a prominent brass lens assembly protruding from the front, and a secondary brass tube mounted above, likely for focusing or as a viewfinder. The sides reveal a black accordion-style bellows, allowing the camera to extend and contract for focusing. The rear of the camera is flat and appears to have a ground glass focusing screen, framed by wood, with a sliding brass latch at the top. The base consists of a wooden frame with rails, suggesting it was designed to be placed on a sturdy surface or possibly mounted on a stand. The overall construction is robust, with visible brass fittings and a rich, polished wood finish.
description: A large, antique wooden box camera with brass lens fittings, black bellows, and a sturdy wooden frame, evoking early photographic equipment.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main brass lens, secondary upper tube, and detailed wood paneling.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended black bellows and the depth of the camera body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat ground glass focusing screen and wooden frame.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with bellows extended and both brass lens tubes visible.
